content:
- Continued task 02 (migrate parser/defaults/validation to explicit enum-driven config semantics). Task file was in TO BE VERIFIED; did a skeptical review with parallel subagents and changed plan (locked v1 policy to fail-closed + reduced v2 schema refactor scope) then flipped marker to NOW EXECUTE.
- Implemented v2 config parsing end-to-end: require explicit `config_version`, reject v1 with migration guidance, parse v2 via new `RuntimeConfigV2Input` wrapper structs (Option leaves for secure fields) and normalize into `RuntimeConfig` with only safe defaults (timeouts/logging/api listen/debug).
- Removed sensitive inference from `src/config/defaults.rs` (no synthesized users/roles/TLS posture/pg_hba/pg_ident); defaults module now only exposes safe defaults + process normalization.
- Hardened `validate_runtime_config` with additional invariants: role usernames non-empty, TLS identity required when mode != disabled (api+postgres), RoleTokens requires at least one token, role password secret sources must be non-empty.
- Updated parser tests: v2 happy path, missing config_version rejected, v1 rejected, unknown v2 fields rejected, actionable missing secure fields. Also fixed a clippy bool comparison lint.
- Gates all green (re-run after final message tweaks): `make check`, `make lint`, `make test`, `make test-long`.
- Marked task 02 as done/passing in `.ralph/tasks/.../02-...md`, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, committed with required message, and pushed.
- Added an AGENTS.md learning: `ConfigError::Validation` uses `field: &'static str`, so v2 parsing benefits from Option-leaf input wrappers + explicit validation for full field paths.

- Next: pick up the next task in story-secure-explicit-node-config (likely task 03 or the one selected by task_switch) and follow the same TO BE VERIFIED -> NOW EXECUTE workflow.

- Read previous progress log.
- Read `.ralph/current_tasks.md` and searched for any `<passes>false</passes>`, `<passing>false</passing>`, or `<passes>meta-task</passes>` markers.
- Confirmed all bug tasks currently show `<passes>true</passes>` / `<passing>true</passing>`.
- Found multiple non-bug tasks with `<passes>false</passes>`, mainly in `story-secure-explicit-node-config` (tasks 03-06).
- Selected the next logical task as `story-secure-explicit-node-config` task 03 to continue the secure explicit config chain.
- Wrote chosen task path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Next: run the Do-Task agent on task 03 and ensure `make check`, `make test`, `make lint` all pass.

- Investigated replication/follow credential wiring for task 03 research.
- Confirmed replication clone path uses runtime/node::default_leader_conninfo() which clones process_defaults.rewind_source_conninfo, and process_defaults_from_config() only sets host/port. User/dbname/ssl stay from contract_stub (postgres/postgres/prefer).
- Confirmed ProcessJobKind::BaseBackup and ProcessJobKind::PgRewind both receive source_conninfo externally; no role-kind enforcement in process layer and no RoleAuthConfig-to-runtime auth wiring (no PGPASSWORD/env injection for password auth).
- Key next step: split process defaults into explicit conninfo per purpose (replication vs rewind), map replication to roles.replicator and rewind to roles.rewinder, and add guard tests that reject accidental postgres/superuser fallback in replica clone jobs.

- Investigated rewind credential paths for task 03 reconnaissance.
- Mapped end-to-end: HA decision emits StartRewind -> HA dispatch builds PgRewindSpec from process_defaults.rewind_source_conninfo -> process worker renders --source-server conninfo for pg_rewind.
- Found concrete bug: runtime process_defaults_from_config only copies rewind host/port and leaves user/dbname/ssl from contract_stub defaults (postgres/postgres/prefer), so cfg.postgres.rewind_conn_identity and cfg.postgres.roles.rewinder are ignored in runtime rewind path.
- Found structural gap: RoleAuthConfig for superuser/replicator/rewinder is parsed/validated but unused in runtime command execution, so rewinder-only auth is not enforced by code.
- Also found coupling: startup clone/basebackup path uses default_leader_conninfo(process_defaults.rewind_source_conninfo), so replication clone currently shares rewind identity defaults.
- Next if executing: wire explicit role-mapped conninfo/auth builder and type-safe role-bound specs/tests.

- Scanned codebase for PostgreSQL credential/role selection wiring for task 03.
- Found roles represented in config schema/parser, but runtime selection currently uses generic conn identities and a single rewind_source_conninfo default seeded from contract_stub postgres user.
- Identified key update points: runtime/node process_defaults_from_config + default_leader_conninfo, ha/state ProcessDispatchDefaults, ha/worker StartRewind dispatch, process/jobs specs, process/worker command/env handling, and parser cross-field invariants.
- Next: implement role-kind based mapping + enforce role-specific username/auth mode at all connection builder call sites, then update tests and run full gates.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> selected task 03: enforce role-specific credential usage across runtime.
- Researched current credential/conninfo wiring with parallel explorer subagents + local `rg`/file reads.
- Found key issue: `ProcessDispatchDefaults::contract_stub()` hardcodes remote conninfo user/dbname to `postgres`, and runtime only overwrites host/port; both basebackup clone and pg_rewind paths inherit this (role-misuse / superuser fallback).
- Wrote a detailed end-to-end plan into `.ralph/tasks/story-secure-explicit-node-config/03-task-enforce-role-specific-credential-usage-across-runtime.md` and appended the required `TO BE VERIFIED` marker.

- Next: reopen task 03 file, do DEEP+SKEPTICAL verification of the plan (must change at least one thing), then replace marker with `NOW EXECUTE` and implement without further exploration; ensure `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint` all pass.

- Inspected config schema/parser and runtime/process consumers for postgres role/auth identity invariants.
- Confirmed parser enforces presence/non-empty for local_conn_identity/rewind_conn_identity and roles/auth secret shape, but no cross-field equality invariants between identity.user and role.username.
- Confirmed runtime/process still consume identity.user and a stub conninfo with user=postgres; role auth is not yet consumed in command execution. Recommended enforcement point: validate_runtime_config cross-field checks now; deprecate identity.user only after runtime migrates to role-derived users.

- Investigated credential propagation paths requested: ProcessDispatchDefaults::contract_stub, default_leader_conninfo, clone/basebackup and rewind flow wiring.
- Confirmed hardcoded/stub behavior: ProcessDispatchDefaults has only rewind_source_conninfo and contract_stub seeds user/dbname as postgres; runtime process_defaults_from_config mutates only host/port leaving user/dbname from stub. default_leader_conninfo clones rewind_source_conninfo, so startup clone/basebackup uses rewind conninfo lineage.
- Confirmed HA rewind path: HaAction::StartRewind uses ctx.process_defaults.rewind_source_conninfo directly.
- Confirmed process layer accepts untyped PgConnInfo for both BaseBackupSpec and PgRewindSpec; build_command hardcodes initdb -U postgres, and TokioCommandRunner has no env plumbing for role password auth.
- Confirmed local SQL DSN currently uses postgres.local_conn_identity.user (not roles.superuser.username) and validate_runtime_config currently does not enforce identity/role equality invariants.
- Next if executing fix: split defaults for clone vs rewind conninfo, build conninfo from explicit role mapping, add bootstrap superuser username in job spec, add process env support for password auth, and add config cross-field validation/tests.

- Inspected process layer files: src/process/jobs.rs, src/process/worker.rs, src/process/state.rs, src/pginfo/conninfo.rs, and related upstream wiring in src/runtime/node.rs + src/ha/state.rs.
- Confirmed process command model has no env support today: ProcessCommandSpec only has program/args/capture_output/log_identity, and TokioCommandRunner::spawn only applies args/stdin/stdout/stderr (no command.env).
- Found hardcoded bootstrap superuser in build_command Bootstrap path (`initdb ... -U postgres`). This bypasses configured role usernames.
- Found BaseBackup and PgRewind specs carry raw PgConnInfo, so process layer cannot enforce role-specific typing; whatever upstream passes is used.
- BaseBackup path does basic host/user non-empty validation; PgRewind path does not validate source_conninfo fields before rendering --source-server.
- PgConnInfo itself has no auth material; conninfo render includes user/dbname/ssl/options but no password/cert handling. Combined with no env support means RoleAuthConfig cannot be applied in process runner.
- Noted upstream risk feeding process jobs: ProcessDispatchDefaults contract stub hardcodes rewind_source_conninfo user=postgres and runtime default_leader_conninfo clones rewind_source_conninfo for clone/basebackup startup source.
- Test coverage gaps in process layer: no unit test for build_command PgRewind conninfo mapping and no test asserting bootstrap user is configurable; existing tests include postgres assumptions in sample helper/probes.
- Next for parent: implement typed role-specific specs + env plumbing and adjust tests/fixtures to distinct usernames.

- Investigated test/BDD/harness usage of postgres hardcoding for runtime config and process jobs.
- Found concrete hardcoded assumptions in process command building/tests (initdb -U postgres in build_command and harness initdb helper), runtime defaults wiring (rewind conninfo reused for basebackup), and probe helpers using psql -U postgres.
- Next: report exact file+line hotspots and propose specific fixture/test updates: distinguish role usernames in fixtures, add role-mapping unit tests for runtime dispatch and process command generation (bootstrap/basebackup/rewind), and parameterize harness psql user/db.

- (worker B) Updated `src/ha/state.rs` `ProcessDispatchDefaults` to split remote conninfo: added `basebackup_source_conninfo` and kept `rewind_source_conninfo`.
- (worker B) Updated `src/runtime/node.rs` to build `ProcessDispatchDefaults` from scratch (no more mutate `contract_stub()`), and mapped usernames to roles: basebackup uses `postgres.roles.replicator.username`, rewind uses `postgres.roles.rewinder.username`.
- (worker B) Updated runtime local pginfo DSN to use `postgres.roles.superuser.username` (not `local_conn_identity.user`), while still using identity dbname/sslmode.
- (worker B) Updated clone startup leader conninfo to derive from `basebackup_source_conninfo`.
- (worker B) Updated HA dispatch (`src/ha/worker.rs`) to pass `BootstrapSpec.superuser_username` from config and updated test helpers for new defaults.
- Added unit test in `src/runtime/node.rs` asserting role-specific user mapping for local DSN, clone/basebackup source, and rewind source.
- Next: integrate with process-layer changes (BootstrapSpec field + typed conn wrappers) and update config validations/fixtures + harness, then run make check/test/test-long/lint.

- Updated `src/process/jobs.rs`: added typed role source wrappers `ReplicatorSourceConn` and `RewinderSourceConn`, added `BootstrapSpec.superuser_username`, and extended `ProcessCommandSpec` with `env: Vec<ProcessEnvVar>`. Added `ProcessEnvValue` (Literal/Secret) and a `ProcessError::EnvSecretResolutionFailed` for secret resolution errors.
- Updated `src/process/worker.rs`: 
  - `TokioCommandRunner::spawn` now applies `spec.env` and resolves `SecretSource` (file/inline) at spawn time.
  - `build_command`:
    - Bootstrap uses configured `superuser_username` (no hardcoded postgres) and validates it non-empty.
    - BaseBackup/PgRewind now consume `spec.source.{conninfo,auth}` and add `PGPASSWORD` env var for `RoleAuthConfig::Password`.
    - Added validation for pg_rewind source host/user/dbname non-empty.
    - Added `env: Vec::new()` to all other job kinds.
- Updated process unit/real tests in `src/process/worker.rs` to use new spec shapes and added new unit tests ensuring `PGPASSWORD` is set for password auth and passwords do not leak into args.
- Avoided unwrap/expect/panic in new code (converted new unit tests to `-> Result<(), String>`).
- Next: parent agent must update all non-process call sites for `BootstrapSpec`, `BaseBackupSpec`, `PgRewindSpec` and any code that expects `ProcessDispatchDefaults.rewind_source_conninfo` etc. Also any code building `ProcessCommandSpec` directly now needs the `env` field (search found only `build_command` + spawn destructure).

- Implemented cross-field config validation: local_conn_identity.user must equal roles.superuser.username; rewind_conn_identity.user must equal roles.rewinder.username (src/config/parser.rs).
- Updated all listed RuntimeConfig fixtures to satisfy new validation by setting rewind_conn_identity.user to "rewinder" where roles.rewinder.username == "rewinder" (api/dcs/debug/ha/worker_contract_tests/tests/bdd/etc).
- Refactored ha_e2e harness psql helper to accept username+dbname (src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs) and threaded configured superuser identity through TestClusterHandle (new fields superuser_username/superuser_dbname) + startup guard.
- Updated call sites in test harness and HA real e2e drivers (src/test_harness/ha_e2e/ops.rs, src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs, src/ha/e2e_partition_chaos.rs) to pass configured superuser credentials.
- Updated logging/postgres_ingest real-binary tests to compile with new process job specs (BootstrapSpec.superuser_username, BaseBackupSpec.source=ReplicatorSourceConn + RoleAuthConfig::Tls).
- Verified partial compile: remaining errors are in src/runtime/node.rs and src/ha/worker.rs due to BaseBackupSpec/PgRewindSpec field rename (to be fixed by runtime/ha owner).
